mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate earnings prospects, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.5%, or signal loses predictive power (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer spending and construction activity, generating positive equity returns as corporate revenue growth accelerates.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a statistically significant positive return differential (t-test p > 0.05) compared to periods where only one series rises.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rising inflation above 2.0% annually, the Federal Reserve tightens policy expectations, increasing discount rates and triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in >50% of high-inflation observations, or the signal's predictive accuracy vanishes when CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.0% but FEDFUNDS remains unchanged.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly correlates with higher corporate profit margins and positive equity market returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative or below 0.01 across all rolling 2-year windows, or INDPRO growth fails to predict return direction with >55% accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment gains exceeding 200,000 over a three-month window confirm robust labor demand and wage growth, supporting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative forward returns in >45% of triggered cases, or the 200k threshold shows no statistical edge over a 100k baseline in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates while inflation remains elevated, stagflationary pressures erode real corporate earnings and force monetary tightening, producing negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive returns in >60% of observations, or the interaction term loses significance (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-unemployment environment below 5.0%, rising industrial production signals strong demand-pull dynamics without labor market bottlenecks, generating immediate positive equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in >50% of triggered instances, or the signal's predictive power disappears when UNRATE exceeds 5.0% despite rising INDPRO.
